titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Edward Mycue Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday August 1, 2019 at 03:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material is open for research.
Boxes 114-116 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 117-118 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Bolerium Books on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2011.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into one grouping: August 2011 Acquisition.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Edward Mycue (1937- ) is an American poet born in Niagara Falls, New York. Mycue was a MacDowell Colony Fellow in 1974 and has published over a dozen volumes of poetry.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Edward Mycue Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below and titled according to month and year of acquisition.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Edward Mycue Papers, which span from 1950-2011, contain extensive correspondence, writings, artwork, working files, notes, clippings, audiovisual and born digital materials, along with other documentation related to his life and work.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Edward Mycue Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Writings, business and personal papers, and artwork

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains writings, drafts, notes, correspondence, working and project files, printed material, artwork, and other documentation related to Edward Mycue's life and work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes inscribed Brian Monte manuscript, family correspondence, and rejection letters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with James Broughton and various academics.

did.unittitle: Correspondence with Carol Schneck and Charlie Nash
Correspondence with Carol Schneck and Charlie Nash
Correspondence with Carol Schneck, Charlie Nash, Laura Kennelly (poet and sports journalist)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains manuscripts of writings by Mycue including Damagers, Assault on Summer, and Muddy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains manuscripts of writings by Mycue including Damagers, Assault on Summer, and Muddy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains manuscripts of writings by Mycue including Damagers, Assault on Summer, and Muddy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes publishing files and correspondence with DJ Powell, Will Inman, and Simon Perchik.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Lawrence Fixel, Laura Kennelly, Jack Gilbert, John Gill, and Bruce McPherson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Lawrence Fixel, Laura Kennelly, Jack Gilbert, John Gill, and Bruce McPherson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Lawrence Fixel, Laura Kennelly, Jack Gilbert, John Gill, and Bruce McPherson.

did.unittitle: Correspondence including acceptances and letters from editors
Correspondence including acceptances and letters from editors

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Includes publications Mycue worked on Pajandrum Books, published by Dennis Koran.

did.unittitle: Magazines with Mycue contributions, papers on local literary life, and correspondence
Large pieces, posters, and artwork by Richard Steger

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes some correspondence.

did.unittitle: Posters for readings and other events
Posters for readings and other events
Posters for readings and other events
Posters for readings and other events
Mycue working files, drafts, and poems
Mycue working files, drafts, and poems
Mycue working files, drafts, and poems
Mycue working files, drafts, and poems
Norton Coker and Mother's Hen press manuscripts with related correspondence
Typed and photocopy versions of poems, drafts, and rejections
Typed and photocopy versions of poems, drafts, and rejections

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Will Inman, Laura Riding Jackson, Ann Stanford, Meanjin, No Apologies, and others.

did.unittitle: Poems, small press books, and alternative publications

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Edward Field and many others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Edward Field and many others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with George Oppen and James Merrill.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with George Oppen and James Merrill.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains writings from members of a poetry group run by Mycue. Includes photocopies of numerous poems from 1970-2000, some unpublished, by authors including Nanos Valoritis and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes mostly poetry correspondence, rejections, calendar-journals, personal papers, notes, and other documenents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes documents related to James Broughton, Cid Corman, Jack Foley, Dale Blair, David Gascoyne and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes documents related to James Broughton, Cid Corman, Jack Foley, Dale Blair, David Gascoyne and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes documents related to Lawrence Fixel.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes papers related to Norton Coker and the Took magazine project.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes published versions of Mycue poems from magazines and other publications.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes published versions of Mycue poems from magazines and other publications.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes published versions of Mycue poems from magazines and other publications.

did.unittitle: Submission logs that note acceptances and rejections
Submission logs that note acceptances and rejections
Submission logs that note acceptances and rejections
Submission logs that note acceptances and rejections
Correspondence, acceptances and rejections, and chain art
Correspondence, acceptances and rejections, and chain art
Correspondence, acceptances and rejections, and chain art

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "X-Ray of Photo of Ed Mycue" by Richard Steger, "Damage" by Richard Steger, print on canvas, and "Clearing Winter Storm, Yosemite National Park, Ca., 1944" by E. H. Williams, Dec. 2000.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes cover art for Gypsy Table and collage by B. Newman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes posters for "Munster Poster," "Bibliography 3," and "Analogical Aesthetics 6."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes two unidentified portraits.
